commit.message:
feat: persistent image history with local storage

- Images downloaded from BFL and saved to ./images/ with metadata JSON
- Auto-persist on generation completion (fire-and-forget)
- GET /api/history lists all saved generations (newest first)
- DELETE /api/history?id=X removes image + metadata
- GET /api/images/[filename] serves saved images with caching
- POST /api/persist downloads and saves image + metadata
- History page with grid view, model badges, dates, delete, expand
- All generate/edit/demo pages pass prompt/model metadata

commit.message:
feat: full interactive demo - onboarding + math + PC help in Hebrew

Real recording of complete Squad for Kids experience as child 'אורי' (age 10):

Onboarding flow (reads each response, replies correctly):
1. 'I want to build a cool game!' → Squad welcomes, asks name
2. 'אורי' → Squad asks birthday
3. '15/03/2015' → Squad asks universe theme
4. 'גיבורי על! 🦸' → Squad builds superhero learning team
5. 'Superhero saving the world from monsters!' → Squad starts project

Beyond games - real learning:
6. 'How much is 247×18?' → Squad teaches step-by-step (4446 ✅)
7. 'My PC is slow, what to do?' → Squad gives 6-step troubleshooting guide

Full-screen chat, Autopilot mode, 3:02, 1280x720, 1.8MB
100% real Hebrew interaction, zero fakes

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Verify Agent TARS report against cloned source code

Major corrections:
- Context Engineering: L0-L3 tiers NOT found in source; rewritten to
  reflect actual WorkspacePack/ContextReferenceProcessor/ImageProcessor
- Event Stream: added verified type names (AgentEventStreamProcessor,
  CoreEventType with actual event names)
- Browser strategies: added verified class names (BrowserHybridStrategy,
  BrowserVisualGroundingStrategy, BrowserDOMStrategy)
- Inheritance chain: verified Agent → MCPAgent → AgentTARS
- gui-agent/: expanded from 3 to 8 packages (added agent-sdk, cli,
  operator-nutjs, operator-adb, operator-aio)
- omni-tars/: expanded to 5 sub-packages (core, omni-agent, mcp-agent,
  gui-agent, code-agent)
- Build instructions: multimodal uses pdk not turbo; corrected to
  pnpm bootstrap + pnpm dev workflow
- Added Node.js version inconsistency note (root >=20, multimodal >=22)
- Updated dependency graph with @tarko/agent-snapshot, @agent-tars/interface
- MCP SDK version corrected to ^1.12.1 in mcp-agent
- Updated confidence assessment with source-verified ratings

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

repository.description: Tech insights and analysis

commit.message:
feat: add multi-squad hub + sub-squad monitoring (Phase 1 + 2)

Phase 1 - Sub-Squad Support:
- data-reader.js: new readSubSquads() scans .squad/sub-squads/{name}/ for nested .squad/ dirs
- html-generator.js: conditional Sub-Squads tab with expandable team roster, agents, decisions
- Dashboard shows sub-squad count in stats grid when sub-squads exist

Phase 2 - Multi-Squad Hub:
- New src/config-reader.js reads optional squads.config.json with squad entries
- build.js: generates dist/hub.html + dist/squads/{id}/index.html when config exists
- serve.js: hub at /, individual squads at /squads/:id, timestamp aggregates all roots
- Hub uses Pico.css dark theme, clickable squad cards with agent/decision counts
- All inline scripts use string-array-join pattern (no template literal conflicts)

Fully backward compatible: without squads.config.json, single-squad mode unchanged.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

1. Sidious: Skills-vs-charters alignment audit → 30 charter updates needed
2. Plagueis: Charter quality review → B+ grade, 5 priority fixes identified
3. Nihilus: Eval baseline framework → Priority 1-3 measurement strategy

Artifacts created:
- .squad/orchestration-log/2026-03-22T14-10-04Z-sidious-reskill-conductor.md
- .squad/orchestration-log/2026-03-22T14-10-05Z-plagueis-prompt-quality-review.md
- .squad/orchestration-log/2026-03-22T14-10-06Z-nihilus-eval-baseline-assessment.md

Decisions merged into .squad/decisions.md:
- D-SKL-001: Standardized "Skills & Tools" section in charters
- D-SKL-002: Skill confidence thresholds (⚠️ markers for experimental)
- D-SKL-003: Skill discovery protocol in .squad/skills/README.md

commit.message:
Release v0.1.25

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: narration must explain concept, not narrate visuals

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: PRB Generator — full symmetric partial-create recovery

Handles all 4 partial-success scenarios:
1. Both succeed         → full create + link (unchanged)
2. Defect ✓ Story ✗    → show 'Retry Story Only' button pre-filled with defect key
3. Story  ✓ Defect ✗   → show 'Retry Defect Only' button pre-filled with story key
4. Both already exist   → guard: error if both existing keys are filled

'Existing Defect' and 'Existing Story' input fields on the Create form:
- Fill one to skip that issue's creation and go straight to create+link
- Clear both for normal full creation flow
- Button label updates to reflect mode (Defect Only / Story Only / Both)
- prbRetryStoryOnly() mirrors prbRetryDefectOnly() symmetrically

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: remove duplicate export default causing blank page crash

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: PRB auto-fix string→option format error + Retry Defect Only button

prbCreateIssue() now handles two auto-fix types in one retry pass:
- 'not on the appropriate screen' → field removed from payload
- 'no String-argument constructor from String value' → field auto-converted
  from plain string to {value: string} (fixes customfield_10253 = 'Production')

prbRetryDefectOnly(storyKey): new function for the partial-success case
where story was created but defect failed. Shows 'Retry Defect Only' button
pre-filled with the existing story key — creates only the defect and links it.

PRB._lastCreate stores {defectPayload, storyPayload, linkType, jiraUrl} so
retry has full context without re-fetching.

commit.message:
Implement multiplayer visibility and attacking for new players

- Add Colyseus client (colyseus.js) for real-time server connection
- Create NetworkService to manage connection, state sync, and input sending
- Create RemoteTank entity to render other players with health bars and names
- Create ServerBullet entity to render bullets from server state
- Update GameScene to:
  - Connect to Colyseus server on game start
  - Handle player join/leave events immediately
  - Sync remote player positions, rotations, health
  - Render all bullets from server state
  - Send player inputs to server each frame
  - Display kill/respawn notifications
- Add vitest to server devDependencies (was missing)
- Fix type imports for verbatimModuleSyntax

New players are now immediately visible to all connected players and can be
attacked without any protection period or visibility delay.

Closes #2

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
security: fix CodeQL suppression comment rule IDs

Previous comments used incorrect IDs (js/network-data-written-to-file,
js/file-data-to-outbound-request) which CodeQL did not recognise.
Correct IDs are:
  js/http-to-file-access      — Network data written to file
  js/file-access-to-http      — File data in outbound network request

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: fix TOCTOU race conditions and suppress intentional data-flow alerts

Race conditions (js/file-system-race-condition):
- install.service: replace fs.access() + conditional writeFile() with atomic
  writeFile({ flag: 'wx' }) + catch EEXIST for NeoForge and Fabric version
  manifest writes — eliminates the check-then-use window
- modpack-api.service: replace fs.stat() + fs.readFile() with a single
  FileHandle (open → stat → readFile on the same handle) so the file cannot
  be deleted between the existence check and the read

False positives (js/network-data-written-to-file, js/file-data-to-outbound-request):
- install.service: suppress writeFile alerts for pack manifest/versionManifest
  writes — a modpack launcher intentionally persists fetched pack data
- modpack-api.service: suppress writeFile alert for logo image cache write
- launch.service: suppress fetch body alert for user-triggered log upload
  to paste.myftb.de

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: manim concept layout — force group+center pattern in prompt, explicit 854x480 resolution, camera setup injection

commit.message:
Release v0.1.24

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: PRB Generator — correct Jira field format for option, priority, issuelink

prbFormatFieldValue corrections:
- option:    {name:val} -> {value:val}  (Jira custom selects use 'value', not 'name')
- priority:  numeric values now sent as {id:val} instead of {name:val}
             (stored value '4' is a Jira ID, not a priority name)
- issuelink: {key:val} -> plain string  (Epic Link / Feature Key expects bare key)
- array:     items now {value:} instead of {name:} for multi-select custom fields

prbInferSchemaType() helper:
- If Jira reports schema.type='string' but field has allowedValues, infer 'option'
  (fixes customfield_10253 and similar misdetected fields on future imports)
- Detects epic-link custom fields and maps to 'issuelink'

Action required for existing templates:
- customfield_10253 stored as 'string' type needs to be changed to 'Option'
  in Field Builder > Settings (one-time fix for existing saved templates)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Release v0.1.23

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: re-add Check import to Concepts.tsx (was used but accidentally removed)

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

feat: replace fake steps with cooking animation on Concepts page

Consistent UX with Premium mode — same spinning chef animation instead of
the step-by-step checklist.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: exclude build artifacts from CodeQL scan, remove unneeded build step

CodeQL analyses TypeScript source directly and does not need a compiled
build. Running the build before analysis caused out/ and dist/ to be
included in the scan, producing false positives from bundled/minified
output. Remove the build step and add paths-ignore for out/, dist/, and
node_modules/ to keep the scan scoped to src/.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

security: fix CodeQL warnings across install, java, API, and router services

- install.service: add isPathWithinDir() guard to reject manifest task.to
  paths that escape the instance directory via ../ traversal (js/path-injection)
- modpack-api.service: escape prop before inserting into new RegExp() to
  prevent regex injection (js/regex-injection)
- java.service: replace dynamic new RegExp() with a static Record<17|21,RegExp>
  lookup — required is always a number but CodeQL flags it (js/regex-injection)
- router.ts: add explicit https:/http: protocol check before shell.openExternal
  to silence open-redirect alert (js/open-url-redirection)
- launch.service: strip newlines from packName before writing desktop entry
  fields as a defense-in-depth measure
- tests: add install-path-guard.test.ts (7 cases for isPathWithinDir) and
  extend java-version.test.ts with two cross-version rejection cases

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

chore: block all major version bumps in Dependabot

A vite 7→8 PR broke the build because electron-vite doesn't support
Vite 8 yet. The previous config only blocked majors for electron-vite
and @vitejs/plugin-react but not vite itself.

Replace the per-package ignore list with a global rule that blocks all
major bumps. Major upgrades always require manual migration work anyway
and should not be opened automatically.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

fix: PRB auto-strip screen-incompatible fields and retry

When Jira rejects fields with 'not on the appropriate screen', automatically
strip those fields and retry the create — fully transparent to the user.

On success, shows which fields were auto-removed (e.g. customfield_10265,
customfield_10254 on Defect; customfield_10253 on Story).
On retry failure, debugBlock shows the stripped payload actually sent.

commit.message:
fix: PRB Generator — robust error display with full Jira response

- Rewrote prbCreate() promise chain: defect and story errors captured independently
  (previously, defect failure would shadow story error or swallow it entirely)
- Added 'Show Jira response' debug collapse alongside payload debug in error display
- New captureError() preserves e.responseData from relay error objects
- Added console.log for all payloads sent and responses received
- console.error with full response data on any Jira API rejection
- Error display now shows both the formatted error AND the raw Jira response body
  so users can see exactly WHY their payload was rejected
